title: Unit fractions on the number line
description: Use unit fractions to think about the location of other fractions on the number line.
topic_path: Unit fractions on the number line

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $\dfrac{1}{6}$ on the number line.**  
*First, change the number of divisions to create tick marks on the number line.*

[[☃ number-line 2]]
hints:
$\dfrac16$ has a denominator of $6$, so we need to divide the whole into $6$ equal parts.


[[☃ image 1]]

Each division represents a length of $\dfrac16$.
We need to move  $\dfrac16$ on the number line $\blueD1$ time to reach $\dfrac16$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

The number line should look like this:


[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number is at point $\blueD{A}?$**

[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The length of the number line is $5$ fourths.

[[☃ image 1]]
Each division represents a length of $\dfrac14$.

We need to move  $\dfrac14$ on the number line $\blueD1$ time to reach the point.  

[[☃ image 1]]
Point $\blueD{A}$ is at $\blueD{\dfrac14}$.

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, a tick mark at point A, 3 unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at five fourths.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number is at point $\blueD{A}?$**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The length of the number line is $7$ fourths.


[[☃ image 1]]
Each division represents a length of $\dfrac14$.

We need to move  $\dfrac14$ on the number line $\blueD1$ time to reach the point.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Point $\blueD{A}$ is at $\blueD{\dfrac14}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to seven-fourths with unlabeled tick marks every one-fourth unit. Point A appears at the first tick mark after the 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number is at point $\blueD{A}?$ **


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The length of the number line is $4$ halves.

[[☃ image 1]]
Each division represents a length of $\dfrac12$.

We need to move  $\dfrac12$ on the number line $\blueD1$ time to reach the point.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Point $\blueD{A}$ is at $\blueD{\dfrac12}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to four-halves with unlabeled tick marks every one-half unit. A blue dot labeled A appears at the first tick mark after 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number is at point $\blueD{A}?$**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:

[[☃ image 1]]
Each division represents a length of $\dfrac12$.

We need to move  $\dfrac12$ on the number line $\blueD1$ time to reach the point.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Point $\blueD{A}$ is at $\blueD{\dfrac12}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to five-halves with unlabeled tick marks at every one-half unit. There is a dot labeled A at the first tick mark after 0.
